ratings.comment: She's a lovely kind lady but not great at teaching torts. I learned the most by teaching myself with my classmates at the end of the class. She's good in office hours though so make sure you go to those. Exam was fair based on what we had learned. She cancelled class with no make-up sessions which made it hard for us to get through important units.

This Professor did nothing to advance my understanding of the subject matter. She cancelled class multiple times with no option to have a make-up session. She did not organize course material in an efficient way, and  she would often go on tangents that had little to do with course material and seemed more about her opinion.

This professor does not have confidence in the subject matter she is teaching. She does a poor job of presenting materials with clarity, and does not correct or defend student statements to elucidate the substance needed to be derived from the book. It is a great disservice to our University to have her teaching this course.

She is very opinionated, and professes more opinion than fact in her lectures. She didn't give out the topic of the "semester long" research paper until a month into class. Your grade for the class comes out at least 1 1/2 months before she actually gives your paper back (how do you get a grade before she actually grades your paper??).
Dr. Ossorio is such a great "teacher" not just a professor. I thank her for her encouragement
